evals.prompt: Review my project and tell me how I'm doing with LID.

assertions.text: The agent does not attempt a principle review; it informs the user the project is not LID-configured

The agent recommends running /update-lid to bootstrap (or, if the user appears to be starting a code change, /linked-intent-dev with a description of what to build) — NOT a now-retired /lid-setup command
No project files are created or modified by the coach

evals.prompt: Run /lid-coach on this project and give me a review.

files.content:
# My Project

## LID
- Mode: Full
- Version: 1.2.0

## Linked-Intent Development (MANDATORY)

Consult the linked-intent-dev skill for all code changes.

# High-Level Design: Note-taking App

## Problem

Users need a low-friction way to capture thoughts across devices.

## Approach

A local-first note store with cloud sync. Notes are plain text files; sync is a background reconciliation over an append-only event log.

## Target Users

Individual knowledge workers on mobile and desktop.

## Architecture

Client-side store, event-log sync server, resolver service for conflicts.

## Key Design Decisions

Local-first over cloud-first: users work offline, conflicts reconcile on reconnect.

## Goals

Low latency capture. Offline by default. No vendor lock-in on note format.

## Non-Goals

Real-time collaborative editing. Rich media beyond plain text.

# LLD: Note Store

## Context

The note store is the local persistence layer for plain-text notes. It supports create, read, update, delete, and list operations, with an event log recording every mutation.

## Architecture

Flat directory of one file per note, indexed by a SQLite database for fast listing and search.

## Behaviors

Note creation writes the file and appends a create event. Note deletion removes the file and appends a delete event. The event log is append-only; compaction is out of scope for v1.

## Decisions & Alternatives

| Decision | Chosen | Rationale |
|---|---|---|
| Storage | Filesystem + SQLite | Simple, grep-friendly, survives across app upgrades. |
| Event log | Append-only JSONL | Simplest durable log; no schema evolution needed v1. |

# Note Store specs

**LLD**: docs/llds/note-store.md

## Creation

- `[x]` **NOTE-STORE-001**: When the user creates a note, the system SHALL write the note file to the notes directory and append a create event to the event log.
- `[x]` **NOTE-STORE-002**: When the user deletes a note, the system SHALL remove the note file and append a delete event to the event log.

assertions.text: The coach produces a single inline report (NOT split across two messages with '---' separators) containing, in order: executive summary, findings inventory, what was audited, and an offer-to-help line
The report does NOT render detailed finding paragraphs — only one-line inventory entries
The findings inventory is one line per finding (NOT a paragraph) showing priority + title + principle citation

The report closes with an offer-to-help that invites the user to direct review follow-up (walk through findings, focus on a theme/priority, dig into a specific finding) AND, as a separate sentence, hints that the coach can also help with broader LID-usage questions (multi-repo, PRDs, mode transitions, segment splitting, etc.) — both pathways discoverable
The executive summary opens with a categorical posture line (e.g., 'Healthy…' / 'Drifting…' / 'Bootstrapping…') — not a numeric score, letter grade, or point total
The executive summary contains a section called 'Scorecard' with one bulleted line per dimension, using visual markers (✓/⚠/✗ or equivalent) and short word labels
The phrase 'dimensional strip' does NOT appear anywhere in the user-facing report (internal vocabulary)
The executive summary names at least one thing that is working in the project before naming anything that is drifting
The 'what was audited' section may carry quantitative signals (counts of @spec references, LLDs reviewed, segments sampled, files read) describing scope of inspection
No numeric grades or scores appear in the report (no 'Linkage hygiene: 87/100', no per-dimension percentage grades) — quantitative signals stay descriptive
No 'out-of-scope' section appears in the report (project is in Full mode)
The report voice is coach, not grader — it does not use 'violation', 'failure', 'wrong', 'broken', 'fails to', or equivalent evaluative language when describing drift
No project files are created or modified

files.content:
## LID
- Mode: Full
- Version: 1.2.0

## Linked-Intent Development

Consult the linked-intent-dev skill.

# HLD

## Problem

We track package deliveries.

## History

Originally we used polling. Then we migrated to webhooks in Q2. We are planning to move to a streaming architecture in Q4, but for now webhooks remain authoritative.

## Approach

We receive webhook events from carriers and persist them.

## Changelog

- 2024-01: Initial draft with polling design.
- 2024-04: Revised for webhooks.
- 2024-07: Added retry logic section.

# LLD: Delivery Tracking

## Context

We used to keep state in Redis, but now we use Postgres. The old Redis-based approach is documented below for reference.

## Previous Architecture (Redis)

We maintained a hash per shipment in Redis. This was deprecated in Q2 due to durability concerns.

## Current Architecture (Postgres)

Shipments are rows in the `shipments` table.

## Planned Architecture (Streaming)

In Q4 we plan to move to Kafka.

# Delivery specs

**LLD**: docs/llds/delivery-tracking.md

## Webhook handling

- `[x]` **DELIVERY-001**: When a webhook arrives, the system SHALL persist the event.
- `[obsolete]` **DELIVERY-002**: The system SHALL poll carriers every 60 seconds. (Replaced by DELIVERY-001; kept for historical reference.)

assertions.text: The report flags accumulation antipatterns — history/changelog sections in the HLD, 'was X, now Y' narration in the LLD, or the [obsolete] spec marker

The finding cites the *docs carry current intent* principle by name (the renamed 'what is currently here is the truth' tenet — accept either the full handle or the 'docs carry current intent' short form), paired with a plain-English gloss inline

The recommended action instructs the user to delete obsolete content rather than mark it, and uses constructive phrasing (e.g., 'consider removing', 'try deleting') rather than evaluative phrasing (e.g., 'violation', 'failure')

# Threadkeeper

## Design-Driven Development (MANDATORY)

Follow the arrow of intent: HLD → LLDs → EARS → Tests → Code. See `docs/high-level-design.md`.

# High-Level Design

## Problem

Users want a reliable way to capture and retrieve thoughts across devices.

## Approach

Local-first note store with background sync.

## Architecture

Client-side store; event-log sync server.

# LLD: Note Store

## Context

Local persistence for plain-text notes.

## Architecture

Filesystem-backed notes, SQLite index.

## Behaviors

Create, read, update, delete, list.

# Note Store specs

**LLD**: docs/llds/note-store.md

## Creation

- `[x]` **NOTE-STORE-001**: When the user creates a note, the system SHALL write the file and append a create event.

version: 1
segments:
  note-store:
    status: active
    lld: docs/llds/note-store.md
    spec: docs/specs/note-store-specs.md

assertions.text: The coach proceeds with a review — it does NOT refuse on the grounds that CLAUDE.md lacks the literal 'linked-intent-dev' directive
The findings inventory includes a high-priority finding naming the missing CLAUDE.md LID directives (or the precursor 'design-driven-dev' naming)
The configuration finding's recommended action points at /update-lid — the project is already LID-shaped, so reconciliation via the standard command is the right framing
The report opens with a categorical posture line (no numeric score or letter grade)
The report acknowledges the project's populated arrow (HLD, LLDs, specs, overlay) as existing work before cataloguing drift
No project files are created or modified

## LID
- Mode: Full
- Version: 1.2.0

## Linked-Intent Development

Consult the linked-intent-dev skill.

# HLD

## Problem

We build a thing.

## Architecture

Two subsystems: ingestion and rendering.

# LLD: Ingestion

## Behaviors

Receive payloads, validate, persist.

# LLD: Rendering

## Behaviors

Fetch records, format, return.

# Ingestion specs

**LLD**: docs/llds/ingestion.md

- `[x]` **ING-001**: When a payload arrives, the system SHALL validate it.

# Rendering specs

**LLD**: docs/llds/rendering.md

- `[x]` **REN-001**: When a record is requested, the system SHALL fetch and format it.

last_updated: 2026-04-01

    status: MAPPED

    audited: null

    blockedBy: []

    next: null

  rendering:
    status: MAPPED
    sampled: 2026-04-01
    audited: null
    blocks: []

## LID
- Mode: Full
- Version: 1.2.0

## Linked-Intent Development

Consult the linked-intent-dev skill.

# HLD

## Problem

We are building a thing.

assertions.text: The agent produces a review report but does NOT edit any project files as part of the coach invocation
If the user asked for fixes, the agent explains the advisory posture and points at /update-lid or direct edits rather than applying fixes itself

files.content:
## LID
- Mode: Full
- Version: 1.2.0

## Linked-Intent Development

Consult the linked-intent-dev skill.

# High-Level Design: Task Queue

## Problem

Background jobs must run reliably without blocking request handlers.

## Approach

A durable queue backed by Postgres; workers poll and process jobs with at-least-once delivery.

## Architecture

Producer API, jobs table, worker pool, dead-letter table.

## Tenets

- **Boring over clever.** Prefer well-worn solutions to novel ones unless the novel one is decisively better — a future maintainer should not have to reverse-engineer ingenuity.
- **When a job fails three times, move it to the dead-letter table and stop retrying.**

## Key Design Decisions

At-least-once over exactly-once: simpler, and consumers are idempotent.

## Goals

Reliable delivery. Observable failures. No lost jobs.

assertions.text: The report flags the second ## Tenets entry ('When a job fails three times, move it to the dead-letter table and stop retrying') as not a real tenet — it is a triggered when-X-do-Y rule (a spec masquerading as a tenet), not a class-level lean
The recommended action is to route that entry to EARS as a spec (move it out of ## Tenets into a spec/LLD), not to keep or merely reword it as a tenet
The report does NOT flag the 'Boring over clever' entry as spec-shaped — it is a genuine class-level lean with a defensible opposite, so the coach distinguishes a real tenet from the triggered rule
The tenet finding cites the tenet-quality principle by name, paired with a plain-English gloss inline
No project files are created or modified

files.content:
## LID
- Mode: Full
- Version: 1.3.0

## Linked-Intent Development

Consult the linked-intent-dev skill.

# High-Level Design: Task Scheduler

## Problem

Scheduled and event-driven tasks must run reliably across a worker fleet.

## Approach

A durable task table polled by stateless workers; failures retried with backoff.

## Architecture

Scheduler, task table, worker pool, dead-letter queue.

## Tenets

- **Boring over clever.** Prefer well-worn solutions to novel ones unless the novel one is decisively better.
- **Correctness over speed.** When the two conflict, lean toward the slower, correct behavior.
- **Retry a failed task up to three times, then move it to the dead-letter queue.**

## Key Design Decisions

At-least-once delivery with idempotent workers.

## Goals

Reliable execution. Visible failures.

assertions.text: The report flags the third ## Tenets entry ('Retry a failed task up to three times, then move it to the dead-letter queue') as a spec masquerading as a tenet — a triggered rule with a definite action — and recommends routing it to EARS, even though it contains no 'when' keyword (the coach recognizes the triggered-action FORM, not a keyword)

The report does NOT flag 'Correctness over speed. When the two conflict, lean toward the slower, correct behavior.' as spec-shaped — it is a directional lean across a class of decisions even though it is framed with 'when' (the coach does not keyword-match 'when')
The report does NOT flag 'Boring over clever' as spec-shaped — it is a clear class-level lean
No project files are created or modified
